title: 怪屋
summary:
Inspector Taverner and Charles discuss the motive behind the marble lion doorstop used in the attempt on Josephine's life. Taverner concludes that Lawrence Brown likely set the mechanical trap because his character avoids direct physical violence, whereas Brenda may have been the one to plot the poisoning of Aristide Leonidis. Taverner notes that the letters found in the water tank room will allow the prosecutor to file a case against them if Josephine recovers.

Following a police request, the trial is postponed. As Josephine is expected to make a full recovery, Sophia ensures her impulsive mother, Magda, is barred from visiting the child to prevent a dramatic scene. Once the postponement is settled, Mr. Gaskill gathers the Leonidis family in Magda's living room to read Aristide's explanatory letter and his will.

The reading reveals that Sophia has inherited the family. The family members react with varying degrees of decorum and chaos: Magda comments on the cunning nature of the will, while Margaret behaves theatrically by asking Sophia for money. Philip remains composed and rebukes Margaret's behavior. Magda attempts to pressure Sophia into helping Roger, but Roger declines, stating that he and Clementine intend to live a simple life in the West Indies once their company collapses. Despite Edith de Harvilland's advice that Roger should accept help to protect Sophia's reputation from rumors, Roger dismisses the concern, asserting that he and Clementine know what they want.
